hypothesis: Training coupled ensembles with the ensemble-consensus SSL on a large out-of-domain unlabeled molecular corpus (e.g., PubChem) while simultaneously enforcing an explicit diversity-promoting regularizer (e.g., negative-correlation loss between member predictions) will yield individual models with significantly lower test MAE on QM9 targets and higher calibration than (i) coupled ensembles trained only on in-domain unlabeled data and (ii) coupled ensembles trained on the same out-of-domain data without the diversity regularizer, while maintaining non-negligible ensemble prediction variance.
